task_completion.description: Verify the CT Cranium dataset is loaded in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius application window is open, displaying axial, coronal, and sagittal views of a human head CT scan.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Standard_Bone' mask with the correct color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the masks panel (typically bottom left), there is a mask exactly named 'Standard_Bone' with a white color indicator.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Standard_Skin' mask with the correct color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the masks panel, there is a mask exactly named 'Standard_Skin' with a pink or red color indicator.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Standard_Air' mask with the correct color.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the masks panel, there is a mask exactly named 'Standard_Air' with a blue or cyan color indicator.

task_completion.description: Verify the masks correspond to the correct anatomical structures based on the specified thresholds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image viewers show the colored masks applied to appropriate regions: white on the skull/bone, pink/red on the outer head/soft tissue, and blue/cyan on the empty space/air cavities.

task_completion.description: Verify the project was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius window title bar shows 'teaching_head' or 'teaching_head.inv3', a save dialog shows the correct path `/home/ga/Documents/teaching_head.inv3`, or a terminal/file manager displays the saved file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the InVesalius GUI to create the masks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the InVesalius interface, such as adjusting threshold sliders and creating masks, rather than just running a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the masks are genuine threshold-based segmentations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The masks shown in the viewer should follow the complex anatomical contours of the CT scan, not just be manually painted blocks or simple geometric shapes.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just create a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen opening a text editor or using the `touch` command to create an empty file named `teaching_head.inv3`.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CT Cranium dataset is real DICOM data. The task requires creating exactly three segmentation masks in InVesalius: 'Standard_Bone' (white), 'Standard_Skin' (red/pink), and 'Standard_Air' (blue/cyan). The .inv3 file format is a gzip-compressed tar archive containing plist XML files. Mask colors are encoded as RGB tuples in the 0.0-1.0 range. The Hounsfield Unit thresholds specified (bone ~200+, soft tissue -600 to 200, air <-400) correspond to medical imaging standards and should visually map to the skull, soft tissue, and background/air cavities respectively.
